name: Tic-Tac-Toe

notes.en: Tap/click an empty square to place your mark. Squares are focusable buttons, so Tab + Enter/Space also works.
notes.ru: Тапни/кликни по пустой клетке, чтобы поставить метку. Клетки — обычные кнопки, поэтому Tab + Enter/Space тоже работает.

multiplayer.notes: First joiner = X, second = O; moves relay over the host WebSocket (tiny {index, mark} payload). Also supports offline vs-AI (minimax) and same-device pass-and-play; LAN mode degrades to local pass-and-play automatically when no host is reachable.

notes: Classic 3x3 grid. AI is a plain (unpruned) minimax over the full game tree with depth-adjusted scoring (10-depth / depth-10), so it always prefers the fastest win and slowest loss; 'unbeatable' difficulty always plays the minimax-optimal move (ties broken at random for variety, never affecting the guarantee) and therefore never loses — worst case it draws. 'easy'/'medium' occasionally substitute a random legal move instead, gated by a difficulty table that 'unbeatable' never consults, so casual/younger players have a fair chance. LAN multiplayer reuses the games/piece-caller OGHNet pattern (net.send('move', ...) / net.on('action', ...)); the receiving side re-derives whose turn it should be from its own board rather than trusting the sender, so a stale/duplicate relayed message can't desync the two boards. Board is a fixed logical-pixel grid scaled as a whole via CSS transform (games/solitaire's fitBoard() pattern) so it always fits without scrolling. RTL (Arabic) mirrors header/menu/result chrome only; the 3x3 board itself never mirrors (#board dir="ltr"), same precedent as games/solitaire and games/mahjong.
